prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response: One song I find genuinely moving is "Mad World" by Gary Jules, a melancholic cover of a Tears for Fears song. I find it utterly captivating. The haunting piano melody evokes the scent of misty rain, and the somber tone precisely captures the overwhelming sensation of "going through the motions" in an almost purposeless world. Even more considerable, I believe the song's meaninglessness is a reaction to the pressures and pretences so intricate to establishing societal bonds which grow to be a palpable ennui. For me, the essence of the song slowly plugs into your brain putting up an existential struggle. As 'all around me are familiar faces, worn-out places,' the song's escapist melancholy is deftly and inactively observed.
